text:
Once upon an autumn evening, young Lily wandered through the antique shop Mrs. Jenkins liked. Rows of old photographs framed her quaint room. Today she sought pictures of her favorite stuffed animal, Hop-o'-Mine-Star.

Mrs. Jenkins noticed Lily's excited gaze. "How about this?" she offered.

A beautiful starfish clumped awkwardly through a lace netting. Though it was battered, it had charm.

"Lovely!" exclaimed Lily. She slid it into her pocket. "Hop?"

"I think you've got Hop." Mrs. Jenkins winked. "Hop loves adventures."

That night Hop floated proudly across Lily's ceiling. The next morning, they woke smiling.

Their world buzzed.

"?You've talked?"

Lily nestled closer. "You love starfish?"

Hop winked. "We love everything!"

They spent mornings exploring nature— starfish scanning rocky shores. By afternoon they dreamed of sailing stars.

One summer their dreams flew through nets—they sailed a tiny boat.
